input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the bottom text input on Android, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach no longer works in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance based on what I know about this change.

In .NET 9+, `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` was deprecated/removed because Android itself deprecated the `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE` mode. The recommended approach in .NET MAUI for .NET 9+ (and continuing into .NET 10) is to use the `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` behavior or handle edge-to-edge insets.


The main solutions involve ensuring `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` is enabled by default in newer MAUI versions, or manually configuring how the app handles window insets when the keyboard appears.
